2026-06-18T18:53:33.9434277Z [36;1mset -euo pipefail[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9434675Z [36;1m# actions/download-artifact (>=v5) flattens contents directly into `path/`[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9435169Z [36;1m# whenever exactly one artifact matches the pattern, ignoring[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9435637Z [36;1m# `merge-multiple: false`. Re-shape into the per-group subdir layout so[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9436138Z [36;1m# downstream validation sees a stable structure regardless of matrix size.[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9436536Z [36;1m# Upstream reference:[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9436958Z [36;1m# https://github.com/actions/download-artifact/blob/v8.0.1/src/download-artifact.ts[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9437533Z [36;1m# (see the `isSingleArtifactDownload || mergeMultiple || artifacts.length === 1`[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9438053Z [36;1m# branch). Remove this step once download-artifact stops flattening or[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9438460Z [36;1m# exposes an opt-out.[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9438808Z [36;1mexpected_count=$(echo "$EXPECTED_MATRIX" | jq '.group // [] | length')[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9439205Z [36;1mif [ "$expected_count" -eq 1 ]; then[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9439556Z [36;1m  group_id=$(echo "$EXPECTED_MATRIX" | jq -r '.group[0].id')[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9440020Z [36;1m  # Defence-in-depth: group_id is interpolated into a shell path. apm-prep[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9440514Z [36;1m  # produces a sanitised id today, but enforce a strict allowlist here so[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9440965Z [36;1m  # any future schema drift cannot smuggle traversal sequences.[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9441393Z [36;1m  if ! printf '%s' "$group_id" | grep -Eq '^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$'; then[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9441837Z [36;1m    echo "::error::unsafe group_id '$group_id' (must match ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$)"[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9444062Z [36;1m    exit 1[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9444275Z [36;1m  fi[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9444597Z [36;1m  group_dir="/tmp/gh-aw/apm-bundles/${ARTIFACT_PREFIX}apm-${group_id}"[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9444977Z [36;1m  if [ ! -d "$group_dir" ]; then[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9445514Z [36;1m    mkdir -p "$group_dir"[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9445985Z [36;1m    find /tmp/gh-aw/apm-bundles -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -path "$group_dir" -exec mv {} "$group_dir/" \;[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:33.9446448Z [36;1m  fi[0m

2026-06-18T18:53:55.4686185Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:55.4686626Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:55.4687050Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:55.4706316Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-06-18T18:53:55.4706798Z [36;1m[0m

2026-06-18T19:01:28.5676209Z {
2026-06-18T19:01:28.5686472Z   "persona": "python-architect",
2026-06-18T19:01:28.5688727Z   "active": true,
2026-06-18T19:01:30.4889178Z   "summary": "Clean minimal fix: top_level_defaults on frozen dataclass correctly injects cursor version:1 on install; sync path silently leaves {version:1} after full uninstall -- document or cover with a test.",
2026-06-18T19:01:30.4891132Z   "findings": [
2026-06-18T19:01:30.4892590Z     {
2026-06-18T19:01:30.4897356Z       "severity": "recommended",
2026-06-18T19:01:30.5392711Z       "summary": "Sync path has no knowledge of top_level_defaults; full Cursor uninstall leaves {\"version\":1} ghost in hooks.json",
2026-06-18T19:01:36.0699302Z       "rationale": "_clean_apm_entries_from_json (L1970) removes APM-tagged hook entries, deletes the event container key when empty, but never touches keys injected via top_level_defaults. After a full install+uninstall cycle: install writes {\"version\":1,\"hooks\":{...APM entries...}}, sync removes all APM entries and del data['hooks'] (L2003-2004), but version:1 is abandoned -- leaving {\"version\":1} in .cursor/hooks.json. Cursor parses this without error (valid empty config), so there is no correctness regression, but APM is asymmetric: it injects on install and never reclaims on uninstall. A user doing `git status` after `apm uninstall` will see a modified hooks.json that APM no longer owns. The behavior should be documented as intentional (schema scaffolding, not APM-owned content) or tested so the expected residual state is explicit.",
2026-06-18T19:01:36.0707530Z       "file": "src/apm_cli/integration/hook_integrator.py",
2026-06-18T19:01:36.0708786Z       "line": 1970,
2026-06-18T19:01:39.3689873Z       "suggestion": "Option A (cheapest): add a comment in _clean_apm_entries_from_json noting that top_level_defaults-injected keys (e.g. 'version') are deliberately left in place because they are schema scaffolding, not APM content markers. Option B: add test_cursor_version_after_full_uninstall that installs cursor hooks, calls sync_integration, then asserts the expected residual state (either version:1 remains, or the file is gone). Either option makes the asymmetry visible and intentional instead of accidental."
2026-06-18T19:01:39.3693570Z     },
2026-06-18T19:01:39.3694014Z     {
2026-06-18T19:01:39.3711226Z       "severity": "nit",
2026-06-18T19:01:39.3745452Z       "summary": "top_level_defaults uses bare dict annotation; dict[str, Any] is more precise",
2026-06-18T19:01:41.5963273Z       "rationale": "Any is already imported at L52. The bare dict annotation conveys nothing about the expected key/value types. dict[str, Any] documents the str->Any contract used in the injection loop at L1416-1418 and makes static analysis tools (mypy, pyright) more useful for this field.",
2026-06-18T19:01:41.5973193Z       "file": "src/apm_cli/integration/hook_integrator.py",

2026-06-18T19:01:49.5548173Z       "component": "```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    A[\"integrate_package_hooks_cursor :1708\"] --> B\n    B[\"_integrate_merged_hooks :1299\"] --> C\n    C[\"[FS] find_hook_files + _filter_hook_files_for_target cursor :1344\"] --> D\n    D{\"hook_files empty? :1346\"}\n    D -- yes --> E[\"return HookIntegrationResult files_integrated=0\"]\n    D -- no --> F[\"[I/O] read .cursor/hooks.json :1372<br>json_config={} on miss or decode-error\"]\n    F --> G[\"seed event container :1409<br>if hooks not in json_config then json_config[hooks]={}\"]\n    G --> H[\"NEW inject top_level_defaults :1416<br>for k,v in config.top_level_defaults.items()<br>if k not in json_config: json_config[k]=v<br>cursor: sets version=1 when absent\"]\n    H --> I[\"for each hook_file:<br>[I/O] _parse_hook_json :1421<br>_rewrite_hooks_data :1426<br>merge entries into json_config[hooks] :1436\"]\n    I --> J[\"[FS] json.dump json_config to .cursor/hooks.json :1670\"]\n    J --> K[\"return HookIntegrationResult files_integrated=N scripts_copied=M\"]\n```"

2026-06-18T19:07:18.0924670Z   "arbitration": "All active panelists agree the core fix is correct. Third, supply-chain-security raises a forward-looking type-bound concern: `top_level_defaults: dict` is too broad and a future contributor could silently expand the write surface; narrowing to `dict[str, int | str | bool]` with a `__post_init__` validator is cheap and keeps the injection surface provably scalar.\n\nThe oss-growth-hacker's side-channel note is strategically accurate: Cursor is the fastest-growing AI editor segment and is prominently featured in APM's README hero line. The `"version": 1` field is automatically emitted for Cursor as part of APM's internal behavior, so users don't need to manually add it themselves. The key finding is that CHANGELOG.md is missing an entry for this fix. Given how many versions were affected and how many users experienced silent breakage, this deserves a `### Fixed` entry under `[Unreleased]` that clearly explains the bug, which versions were impacted, and what the fix does. I'd classify this as recommended severity - the fix itself works, but it's a significant documentation gap that users need to understand what happened.

2026-06-18T19:14:27.5684503Z         "file": "docs/src/content/docs/producer/author-primitives/hooks-and-commands.md",
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5685393Z         "suggestion": "In ## Pitfalls, add: `- **Cursor hooks.json schema.** Cursor requires a top-level \\`\"version\": 1\\` in `.cursor/hooks.json`. APM injects it automatically; do not add or remove it by hand.`"
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5686127Z       }
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5686301Z     ]
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5686488Z   }
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5686657Z   ```
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5686762Z

2026-06-18T19:14:27.5691078Z 
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5691644Z [164] ✓ $ grep -n "version\|assert.*version" /home/runner/work/apm/apm/tests/integration/test_integrators_hooks_execution.py | grep -i "assert\|cursor" | head -20
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5692274Z 
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5692592Z [165] ◐ Now I have a comprehensive view of the PR. Let me analyze the security implications:
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5693108Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5693348Z   1. **`top_level_defaults` in `_MergeHookConfig`**: 
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5693698Z      - This field is a `dict` in a `frozen=True` dataclass
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5694852Z      - However, `frozen=True` only makes the dataclass immutable at the top level (you can't reassign `config.top_level_defaults = ...`), but the dict itself is mutable. A `dict` field with `field(default_factory=dict)` means the dict could technically be mutated after creation via `config.top_level_defaults["key"] = value`.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5697145Z      - In practice, `top_level_defaults` is only populated at module load time with `{"version": 1}` as a compile-time constant, so there's no user-provided input here. But since dicts are mutable, the frozen protection is incomplete—someone could theoretically mutate it despite the frozen dataclass, though there's no evidence of this happening in the codebase.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5698455Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5698681Z   2. **`json_config[key] = value` injection**:
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5699838Z      - The keys and values come exclusively from the module-level constant `_MERGE_HOOK_TARGETS`, with no path from user input, package data, or network sources. The `if key not in json_config` guard prevents overwriting existing user values, and Python dicts don't have prototype pollution vulnerabilities like JavaScript does.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5700934Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5701152Z   3. **Trust model for `.cursor/hooks.json`**:
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5701950Z      - APM now writes a `"version": 1` top-level key if it's missing, alongside the existing hooks data—a benign addition to satisfy Cursor's schema requirements.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5702551Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5702735Z   4. **Bypass risk**:
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5703957Z      - A user could pre-set `"version"` in their `hooks.json` to prevent the default from being applied, but that's intentional behavior, not a security issue. An attacker-controlled `hooks.json` could pre-populate keys matching `top_level_defaults` to skip default injection, but this only prevents APM's defaults from being applied rather than injecting malicious content.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5705215Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5705422Z   5. **Mutable dict in frozen dataclass**:
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5706180Z      - Looking at the `dict` type for `top_level_defaults` in a `frozen=True` dataclass—the dict's conte... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 6148 chars]
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5706674Z 
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5706971Z [166] ✓ view(path: /home/runner/work/apm/apm/src/apm_cli/in..., view_range: [1420, 1445])
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5707295Z 
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5708349Z [167] ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/apm/apm && python3 -c " from dataclasses import dataclass, field @dataclass(frozen=True) class TestFrozen: d: dict = field(default_factory=dict) t = TestFrozen(d={'version': 1}) try: t.d = {'version': 2} print('field reassignment: ALLOWED (frozen broken)') except Exception as e:...

2026-06-18T19:14:27.5740027Z [183] ◐ Now I have a complete picture. Let me verify:
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5740332Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5740924Z   1. `_MergeHookConfig` is only instantiated in `_MERGE_HOOK_TARGETS` (module-level constant, lines 334-368) - all at compile time, no dynamic construction from external input.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5741574Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5742176Z   2. The `top_level_defaults` dict's mutability bypass (frozen dataclass doesn't protect dict internals) - confirmed by the test above.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5742723Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5743504Z   3. Package-provided content flows through `_parse_hook_json()` → `_rewrite_hooks_data()` → extracted as `hooks = rewritten.get("hooks", {})` at line 1437. This path never touches `top_level_defaults`.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5744239Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5744433Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5744716Z   4. The injection at 1416-1418 is safe - compile-time values only.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5745067Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5745278Z   5. No prototype pollution risk in Python.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5745551Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5746273Z   So the change is sound with no blocking findings. The only improvements would be adding type parameters to the `top_level_defaults` dict annotation for better static checking, and there's a second nit that got cut off.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5747055Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5748650Z   Actually, thinking about the mutable dict inside a frozen dataclass - while `frozen=True` doesn't protect the dict's contents themselves, the real risk here is pretty low since these config objects only come from a module-level constant and there's no code path that mutates them after creation. It's more of a theoretical concern for future changes, so I'd call it a recommended improvement rather than just a nit - using `types.MappingProxyType` would be the right pattern to truly lock down the dict.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5750273Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5751189Z   Let me verify the `"version": 1` injection isn't a schema mismatch - that's just a question of whether APM writing an integer versus a string matters, but that's a schema concern, not a security one. The PR is fixing a legitimate bug by adding the missing version field.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5752147Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5753627Z   For the type annotation on `top_level_defaults`, I'll go with recommending `MappingProxyType` or a narrower type hint. My summary is that this is compile-time-only data with no user or network input path, the injection guard is correct, and there's no prototype pollution risk in Python - just two minor quality improvements to note... 2026-06-18T19:14:27.5777363Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5778002Z   What's striking is that there's no CHANGELOG entry for this cursor hooks version fix, despite it being a critical regression that silently broke hooks for all Cursor users across 6 versions.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5778703Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5783451Z   The UX implications are worth considering: there's no visible feedback when `apm install` injects the version field, so existing users won't realize their hooks are now fixed. A brief message would signal that something meaningful happened. The idempotency check is solid though—reinstalls won't overwrite existing values. And it makes sense that only Cursor needs this version requirement; the other editors don't have the same constraint, so the `top_level_defaults` approach is flexible enough to handle editor-specific needs. that really matters for existing users who reinstall - they won't see any indication that their hooks have been repaired. I'm settling on "recommended" for both of these findings since they're usability issues rather than correctness problems, and the panel's role is advisory anyway. The logging question probably falls under the CLI team's scope, but whether users need a visible signal during migration is definitely a DevX concern. Looking at the broader architecture, other targets like codex and gemini don't require version fields, and the design all... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2944 chars]

2026-06-18T19:14:27.5893571Z [216] ◐ Now I have a good picture of the code. Let me analyze the PR:
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5893969Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5894151Z   ## PR Analysis
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5894354Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5894527Z   **What it does:**
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5894976Z   1. Adds a `top_level_defaults: dict = field(default_factory=dict)` field to the `_MergeHookConfig` frozen dataclass.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5895604Z   2. Sets `top_level_defaults={"version": 1}` for the `cursor` entry in `_MERGE_HOOK_TARGETS`.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5896285Z   3. In `_integrate_merged_hooks()`, after seeding the event container, iterates over `top_level_defaults` and injects missing keys.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5896968Z   4. Adds tests: a new assertion in `test_integrate_hookify_cursor`, plus two new test methods.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5897367Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5897542Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5898539Z   5. The approach is architecturally sound — using a frozen dataclass with a dict field via `default_factory` ensures each instance gets its own dict without shared mutable state, though the frozen attribute itself doesn't prevent mutations to the dict contents.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5899455Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5901483Z   6. There's a gap in `_clean_apm_entries_from_json` where it removes the container key if empty but leaves behind injected keys like `"version"`, so after a full uninstall the hooks.json file might retain `{"version": 1}` as a leftover. Looking at what happens when a user uninstalls after having installed cursor hooks: the `_clean_apm_entries_from_json` function removes APM entries from the hooks, and if that empties the hooks object entirely, it deletes the key. But this leaves `{"version": 1}` orphaned in the file since the cleanup logic doesn't know about top-level defaults. It's a minor edge case, but worth considering for a complete production solution.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5903588Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5905149Z   The type annotation for `top_level_defaults` is using a bare `dict` when it should be `dict[str, Any]` for clarity, and since `Any` is already imported, that's a quick improvement. There's also a subtle issue with the frozen dataclass containing a mutable dict—while the reference can't be reassigned, the dict contents can still be modified directly, which could be problematic since these are module-level constants.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5906521Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5907083Z   I'm identifying the architectural patterns here: `_MergeHookConfig` is a frozen dataclass acting as a value object, `... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 13098 chars]
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5907636Z 
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5907930Z [217] ✓ view(path: /home/runner/work/apm/apm/src/apm_cli/in..., view_range: [88, 110])
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5908240Z 
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5908515Z [218] ✓ view(path: /home/runner/work/apm/apm/src/apm_cli/in..., view_range: [46, 56])
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5908814Z 
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5909067Z [219] ◐ Good. `Any` is imported at line 52. Now let me finalize my JSON output.
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5909424Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5910101Z   Let me double-check the sync path once more. In `sync_integration`, the non-Claude targets call `self._clean_apm_entries_from_json` at line 1964. This method (L1970) does NOT know about `top_level_defaults`. 
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5910849Z   
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5911113Z   After full Cursor uninstall:
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5911390Z   - All APM hooks are removed from `hooks`

2026-06-18T19:14:27.5912974Z

2026-06-18T19:14:27.5913885Z [220] ◆ ```json
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5914084Z   {
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5914277Z     "persona": "python-architect",
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5914535Z     "active": true,
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5915328Z     "summary": "Clean minimal fix: top_level_defaults on frozen dataclass correctly injects cursor version:1 on install; sync path silently leaves {version:1} after full uninstall -- document or cover with a test.",
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5916106Z     "findings": [
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5916303Z       {
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5916497Z         "severity": "recommended",
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5917027Z         "summary": "Sync path has no knowledge of top_level_defaults; full Cursor uninstall leaves {\"version\":1} ghost in hooks.json",
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5919977Z         "rationale": "_clean_apm_entries_from_json (L1970) removes APM-tagged hook entries, deletes the event container key when empty, but never touches keys injected via top_level_defaults. After a full install+uninstall cycle: install writes {\"version\":1,\"hooks\":{...APM entries...}}, sync removes all APM entries and del data['hooks'] (L2003-2004), but version:1 is abandoned -- leaving {\"version\":1} in .cursor/hooks.json. Cursor parses this without error (valid empty config), so there is no correctness regression, but APM is asymmetric: it injects on install and never reclaims on uninstall. A user doing `git status` after `apm uninstall` will see a modified hooks.json that APM no longer owns. The behavior should be documented as intentional (schema scaffolding, not APM-owned content) or tested so the expected residual state is explicit.",
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5922759Z         "file": "src/apm_cli/integration/hook_integrator.py",
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5923072Z         "line": 1970,
2026-06-18T19:14:27.5924743Z         "suggestion": "Option A (cheapest): add a comment in _clean_apm_entries_from_json noting that top_level_defaults-injected keys (e.g. 'version') are deliberately left in place because they are schema scaffolding, not APM content markers. Option B: add test_cursor_version_after_full_uninstall that installs cursor hooks, calls sync_integration, then asserts the expected residual state (either version:1 remains, or the file is gone). Either option makes the asymmetry visible and intentional instead of accidental."

Adds a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. Must be the final intended comment — not a placeholder or test value. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"pr":{"description":"Alias for item_number when targeting a pull request. Prefer item_number in new calls.","type":["number","string"]},"pr_number":{"description":"Alias for item_number when targeting a pull request. Prefer item_number in new calls.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 3 label(s) can be removed. Only these labels can be removed: [panel-review panel-approved panel-rejected].","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted. When a bash command is blocked by security policy, call this tool with reason set to \"security\".","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-06-18T19:14:27.7998594Z {"timestamp":"2026-06-18T18:54:33.536Z","event":"rpc_request","_schema":"rpc-message/v2","direction":"OUT","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"method":"get","owner":"microsoft","pullNumber":"1840","repo":"apm"},"name":"pull_request_read"}}}
2026-06-18T19:14:27.8011809Z {"timestamp":"2026-06-18T18:54:34.735Z","event":"rpc_response","_schema":"rpc-message/v2","direction":"IN","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":1840,\"title\":\"fix: emit required \\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;: 1 in .cursor/hooks.json\",\"body\":\"## TL;DR\\n\\n`apm install` (cursor target) was writing `.cursor/hooks.json` without the\\ntop-level `\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;: 1` field that Cursor requires to load project hooks.\\nEvery APM-installed hook was silently dead on arrival. This PR fixes it.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Problem (WHY)\\n\\nCursor\\u0026#39;s hook loader validates the project config against a schema that\\nrequires a top-level `\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;` field. Without it the **entire file is\\nrejected**:\\n\\n```\\nERROR: Invalid project config: Config version must be a number\\nERROR: Failed to parse project hooks configuration\\nNo project hooks configuration found\\n```\\n\\nRoot cause in `_integrate_merged_hooks()`:\\n\\n```python\\njson_config: dict = {}          # starts empty\\nif container not in json_config:\\n    json_config[container] = {}  # only \\u0026#34;hooks\\u0026#34; key is seeded\\n# \\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34; is never written\\n```\\n\\nPackage templates that carry `\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;: 1` have it stripped at parse time\\nbecause `_integrate_merged_hooks()` only reads the `hooks` map from each\\nsource file. This affected **every cursor user with hook-based packages**\\n(APM 0.14.1 -- 0.20.0).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Approach (WHAT)\\n\\nAdd a declarative `top_level_defaults` field to `_MergeHookConfig` that\\nspecifies target-specific top-level keys to inject when absent. Set\\n`{\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;: 1}` for cursor. Inject missing defaults after seeding the\\nevent container.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Implementation (HOW)\\n\\n### `src/apm_cli/integration/hook_integrator.py`\\n\\n1. Add `field` to the `dataclasses` import.\\n2. Add `top_level_defaults: dict = field(default_factory=dict)` to the\\n   `_MergeHookConfig` frozen dataclass.\\n3. Populate `top_level_defaults={\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;: 1}` in the `cursor` entry of\\n   `_MERGE_HOOK_TARGETS`.\\n4. In `_integrate_merged_hooks()`, after seeding the event container:\\n\\n```python\\nfor key, value in config.top_level_defaults.items():\\n    if key not in json_config:\\n        json_config[key] = value\\n```\\n\\nThe `if key not in json_config` guard means reinstalling over a file\\nthat already carries `\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;` is a no-op -- the user\\u0026#39;s value is\\npreserved.\\n\\n### `tests/unit/integration/test_hook_integrator.py`\\n\\n- `test_integrate_hookify_cursor`: added `assert config[\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;] == 1`\\n- `test_cursor_version_emitted_on_fresh_install` (new): fresh `.cursor/`\\n  dir produces `\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;: 1`\\n- `test_cursor_existing_version_preserved` (new): pre-existing\\n  `\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;: 2` survives a reinstall\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\n```mermaid\\nflowchart LR\\n  A[\\u0026#34;_MergeHookConfig\\\\ntop_level_defaults={\\u0026#39;version\\u0026#39;:1}\\u0026#34;] --\\u0026gt; B[\\u0026#34;_integrate_merged_hooks()\\u0026#34;]\\n  B --\\u0026gt; C{key in json_config?}\\n  C -- No --\\u0026gt; D[\\u0026#34;json_config[key] = value\\u0026#34;]\\n  C -- Yes --\\u0026gt; E[\\u0026#34;preserve existing value\\u0026#34;]\\n  D --\\u0026gt; F[\\u0026#34;json.dump to .cursor/hooks.json\\u0026#34;]\\n  E --\\u0026gt; F\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Trade-offs\\n\\n| Decision | Rationale |\\n|----------|-----------|\\n| `top_level_defaults: dict` field on `_MergeHookConfig` | Declarative, co-located with other target config, scales to future targets (codex, windsurf) without code changes |\\n| `if key not in json_config` guard | Idempotent on reinstall; preserves user-set values |\\n| Only cursor gets `{\\u0026#34;version\\u0026#34;: 1}` | Other targets (claude, codex, gemini, windsurf) do not require a schema version field |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Validation evidence\\n\\n- 163 unit tests in `tests/unit/integration/test_hook_integrator.py` -- all pass\\n- `ruff check` -- no diagnostics\\n- `pylint R0801` -- 10.00/10\\n- `lint-auth-signals.sh` -- clean\\n\\nFixes #1823\",\"state\":\"open\",\"draft\":false,\"merged\":false,\"mergeable_state\":\"blocked\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/microsoft/apm/pull/1840\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"sergio-sisternes-epam\",\"id\":207026618,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/sergio-sisternes-epam\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/207026618?v=4\"},\"labels\":[\"panel-review\"],\"re
